questions.q: By what heroic title was Alluri Sitarama Raju popularly known?
questions.explanation: Alluri Sitarama Raju was called Manyam Veerudu, meaning the 'Hero of the Jungle'.

questions.q: Which rebellion did Alluri Sitarama Raju lead?
questions.explanation: Alluri Sitarama Raju led the Rampa Rebellion against colonial authority.

questions.q: Against which colonial laws did Alluri Sitarama Raju mobilise tribal communities?
questions.explanation: He rallied tribal people against the colonial forest laws that restricted their traditional rights.

questions.q: In which mountain range did Alluri Sitarama Raju's rebellion take place?
questions.explanation: The rebellion was waged among the tribal communities of the Eastern Ghats.

questions.q: During which years did the Rampa Rebellion take place?
questions.explanation: The Rampa Rebellion led by Alluri Sitarama Raju lasted from 1922 to 1924.

questions.q: In which year was Alluri Sitarama Raju captured and killed?
questions.explanation: Alluri Sitarama Raju was captured and killed by colonial forces in 1924.

questions.q: Which communities formed the backbone of Alluri Sitarama Raju's rebellion?
questions.explanation: Tribal communities of the hill tracts formed the core of his rebellion against the British.

questions.q: Alluri Sitarama Raju is celebrated as a freedom fighter in which southern region of India?
questions.explanation: He is celebrated as a hero of the Andhra region, where the Rampa hill tracts lie.

questions.q: What style of warfare did Alluri Sitarama Raju mainly use against the British?
questions.explanation: He used guerrilla warfare, launching surprise raids from the forested hills against colonial forces.

questions.q: The phrase 'Manyam Veerudu' describes Alluri Sitarama Raju as the hero of what?
questions.explanation: Manyam Veerudu means 'Hero of the Jungle', reflecting his struggle in the forested hill tracts.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state did Alluri Sitarama Raju's rebellion occur?
questions.explanation: The Rampa Rebellion took place in the hill tracts of present-day Andhra Pradesh.

questions.q: The Rampa Rebellion is also commonly known by which other name?
questions.explanation: The uprising is also called the Manyam Rebellion, after the forested hill region where it was fought.

questions.q: A particular colonial policy restricting tribal practice of shifting cultivation helped spark the revolt. What is that cultivation called?
questions.explanation: Colonial forest laws curbing podu (shifting) cultivation deeply angered the tribal people and fuelled the rebellion.

questions.q: Alluri Sitarama Raju's tactic of raiding police stations was aimed primarily at obtaining what?
questions.explanation: His followers raided police stations chiefly to seize arms and ammunition for their guerrilla struggle.

questions.q: Which two tribal communities were prominent among Alluri Sitarama Raju's followers?
questions.explanation: The Koya and Konda Dora hill tribes were prominent participants in the Rampa Rebellion.

questions.q: Around what year was Alluri Sitarama Raju born?
questions.explanation: Alluri Sitarama Raju is generally believed to have been born around 1897 or 1898.

questions.q: Alluri Sitarama Raju was reportedly influenced by which contemporary national movement, even as he chose armed struggle?
questions.explanation: He was inspired by the ideals of Gandhi's Non-Cooperation Movement, though he turned to armed rebellion when it was called off.

questions.q: What reputation did Alluri Sitarama Raju gain among the tribal people regarding spiritual or mystical powers?
options: He was thought to be an ordinary clerk
He claimed to be a king's son
He was believed to have extraordinary, almost divine powers
questions.explanation: Tribal followers attributed extraordinary, almost mystical powers to him, adding to his charismatic leadership.

questions.q: The first major raids of the Rampa Rebellion began with attacks on police stations in which year?
questions.explanation: The rebellion opened in 1922 with coordinated raids on police stations in the Rampa hill tracts.

questions.q: Why did the British ultimately deploy special paramilitary forces to suppress the rebellion?
options: There were no police in the region
Ordinary police could not match the guerrilla tactics
questions.explanation: Because regular police were unable to counter the rebels' guerrilla raids in the hills, the British brought in special armed forces.

questions.q: Alluri Sitarama Raju is remembered for protecting tribal rights against exploitation by whom, besides the British?
questions.explanation: He stood against the exploitation of tribals by contractors and moneylenders as well as the colonial administration.

questions.q: How was Alluri Sitarama Raju executed after his capture in 1924?
options: Tied to a tree and shot
questions.explanation: After his capture, Alluri Sitarama Raju was tied to a tree and shot dead by colonial forces.

questions.q: Which two British officers killed in September 1922 became symbols of the rebellion's toll on the colonial administration?
questions.explanation: On 24 September 1922 the rebels ambushed and killed British officers Scott-Coward and Hayter near Damanapalli, a notable early blow to colonial forces during the rebellion.

questions.q: The Rampa Rebellion opened in August 1922 with a raid on which police station?
questions.explanation: The rebellion began on 22 August 1922 when Raju's followers raided the Chintapalli police station in the Rampa hill tracts, seizing arms.

questions.q: In which month and year was Alluri Sitarama Raju finally captured and killed?
questions.explanation: Alluri Sitarama Raju was captured and shot in May 1924, bringing the rebellion to an end.

questions.q: Which loyal tribal lieutenant of Alluri Sitarama Raju is also remembered as a key leader of the rebellion?
questions.explanation: Gam Mallu Dora was a prominent tribal lieutenant who fought alongside Alluri Sitarama Raju in the Rampa Rebellion.

questions.q: In his youth Alluri Sitarama Raju was known for undertaking what kind of personal journey before turning to rebellion?
questions.explanation: Before leading the rebellion he led an ascetic life, wandering as a sanyasi and gaining a reputation for spiritual discipline.

questions.q: On the occasion of his 125th birth anniversary in 2022, what did the Indian government do to honour Alluri Sitarama Raju?
options: Held year-long national commemorations and unveiled a statue
questions.explanation: In 2022 the government marked his 125th birth anniversary with year-long commemorations and the unveiling of a large statue in his honour.

questions.q: The colonial Madras Forest Act, a key grievance behind the rebellion, was enacted in which decade?
questions.explanation: The Madras Forest Act of 1882 curtailed tribal access to forests, a long-standing grievance that the rebellion sought to redress.

questions.q: An Andhra Pradesh district was renamed in honour of Alluri Sitarama Raju in which year?
questions.explanation: In 2022 a new district of Andhra Pradesh was named the Alluri Sitharama Raju district in his memory.
